claims.claim: --- ## Key Claims ### Claim 1 - claim: Ayahuasca exhibits MAO-A inhibitory activity via harmala alkaloids, affecting monoamine metabolism - type: mechanism - entities: [ayahuasca, MAOI, harmine, harmaline] - directionality: inhibits metabolism - confidence: low - evidence_strength: theoretical ### Claim 2 - claim: Combining ayahuasca with serotonergic agents (SSRIs, SNRIs, TCAs, certain opioids) may increase risk of serotonin syndrome - type: contraindication - entities: [ayahuasca, SSRIs, SNRIs, TCAs, opioids] - directionality: increases serotonin toxicity - confidence: low - evidence_strength: theoretical ### Claim 3 - claim: Sympathomimetic agents (e.g.

claims.claim: clonidine, guanfacine) may interact with psychedelics through sedation and blood pressure modulation - type: interaction - entities: [clonidine, guanfacine, psychedelics] - directionality: alters sedation and BP - confidence: low - evidence_strength: theoretical ### Claim 6 - claim: Antipsychotics and certain antidepressants may attenuate psychedelic effects via receptor antagonism or downstream signaling effects - type: interaction - entities: [antipsychotics, SSRIs, psychedelics] - directionality: decreases effect - confidence: low - evidence_strength: theoretical --- ## Mechanisms Described - Monoamine oxidase inhibition (MAO-A) - Serotonergic potentiation - Catecholamine accumulation - Glutamatergic modulation (NMDA-related) - Receptor antagonism (5-HT2A blockade) - Hemodynamic modulation (BP/HR changes) --- ## Clinical / Practical Guidance - Avoid combining ayahuasca with serotonergic medications - Avoid stimulants and sympathomimetics - Exercise caution with CNS depressants - Consider washout periods prior to psychedelic use - Monitor cardiovascular responses during administration --- ## Contraindications / Risks - Serotonin syndrome - Hypertensive crisis - CNS depression (additive) - Seizure risk (drug-specific) - Psychiatric destabilization --- ## Interaction Relevance This synthesis suggests interaction relevance across: - antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs, TCAs) - stimulants - opioids (subset) - antipsychotics - antihypertensives - anticonvulsants (e.g.
